items.title: Underwater gardening helping to restore Tasmania's lost kelp forests

children.content: A new live-in internship program on the Tasman Peninsula is working to repopulate the area's endangered giant kelp forests through underwater gardening.

gallery.altText: Video of dappled sunlight shining through giant underwater kelp forest

thumbnail.altText: Video of dappled sunlight shining through giant underwater kelp forest

items.title: 'Right fit': Ken Hinkley announced as inaugural Tasmania Devils coach

children.content: The former Port Adelaide coach is formally announced as the inaugural coach of the Tasmania Devils. It completes a year-long hunt for a senior coach for the AFL's newest club.

image.altText: A man leans against a wooden fence post

items.title: Minister insists Tasmania won't lose health services under sale to Catholic provider

children.content: Doctors are concerned Tasmanians will be forced to travel interstate for certain procedures if the Hobart Private Hospital is sold to Catholic provider Calvary, but the health minister insists there will be no service gap.

image.altText: Exterior signage at Hobart Private Hospital.

items.title: Firebombed synagogue community to address royal commission

children.content: The community from Melbourne's Adass Israel Synagogue will provide evidence at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion today.

image.altText: Three firefighters outside a building which has orange flames coming from the roof at night.

items.title: US launches new strikes as Trump says Iran will 'pay' for soldiers' deaths

children.content: The United States has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran after identifying two soldiers killed while defending against Iranian missile and drone attacks in Jordan.

image.altText: A young female soldier in army greens in front of a US flag.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis declare maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia

children.content: In a statement, the Houthis' military spokesperson Yahya Saree says the group will enforce the blockade against its "criminal Saudi enemy", effective immediately.

image.altText: Yahya Sarea stands in a military uniform speaking at a podium.

items.title: Shared lifestyle might be clue to why some couples have higher dementia risk

children.content: Having a spouse with dementia puts you at a higher risk of developing the condition, a new study finds.

items.title: Israel criticises Labor's draft Palestine policy ahead of party conference

children.content: Israel's embassy has released a lengthy complaint ahead of Labor's national conference on the party's proposed position on Israel and Palestine.

image.altText: A white man with glasses speaks at a lectern in front of a blue background

items.title: Anthony Albanese on the night in parliament he would rather not relive

children.content: From left-wing roots to "radical centre" prime minister, Anthony Albanese reflects on his 30-year career in Parliament House.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands in front of an Australian flag in a grey suit and blue tie looking serious

items.title: Stubborn stigma fuelling shocking violence against sex workers in Australia

children.content: A new inquiry into violence against sex workers in Australia and Thailand comes just weeks after the murder of Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla, allegedly by an Australian man.

gallery.altText: a woman carries a sign saying 'stop mythologising men who kill sex workers'
A frame of a young Thai girl looking at the camera.

items.title: Man charged with murder of UK politician Ann Widdecombe

children.content: Joshua Kerry, 28, will appear in court on Tuesday, UK time, after the death of the prominent conservative politician in her home earlier in July.

image.altText: An older lady speaks to the media.

items.title: Moscow targeted by drones while Russia bombards Kyiv

children.content: Ukraine launches its latest attack on the Russian capital, firing more than 400 drones, while the Kremlin deploys more ballistic missiles against targets across Ukraine.

image.altText: A large smoke plume over a blue sky seen from a suburban street.

items.title: The musical journey of royal wedding cellist Sheku Kanneh-Mason

children.content: Sheku Kanneh-Mason went viral performing at Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding. He's captivated audiences since and is touring Australia this month.

items.title: Quiz: How well do you remember these Australian kids' TV shows?

children.content: What was the name of the tough, mangy cat in The Ferals? And which show featured EC, the faceless doll?

items.title: Why 'narcissist' bosses are more likely to oppose work from home

children.content: Research published last month found narcissistic leaders — those with a tendency to be self-centred and entitled — were more likely than others to resist remote work. Why? The study concluded "it interferes with their motivations for power and status".

image.altText: a man gesturing as he sits in front of a laptop

items.title: Quadruplets born in Brisbane 'one of the rarest births in the world'

children.content: A Queensland family has grown by four after an extremely rare pregnancy with identical quadruplets all sharing one placenta.

gallery.altText: An ultrasound image of four fetuses in utero

thumbnail.altText: An ultrasound image of four fetuses in utero

items.title: Labor MP calls for exclusion zones at synagogues akin to abortion clinics

children.content: Former attorney-general Mark Dreyfus has pushed for more robust social media moderation and protest laws at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.

items.title: Fears massive US private lending market sinking

children.content: The corporate regulator is concerned about the risks posed by private credit for Australians with superannuation.

items.title: New Delhi police clash with protesters as thousands rally for 'cockroach' movement

children.content: Thousands of supporters of India's youth-led "cockroach" movement gathered in New Delhi to march ‌on parliament seeking the resignation of the education minister, despite police denying permission for the protest.

items.title: Hundreds march on Pine Gap in secretive site's biggest protest in decades

children.content: About 300 people have marched on Pine Gap in Alice Springs, protesting against its role in US military operations, in the largest protest at the top-secret defence site since 1987.

image.altText: A crowd of protesters with police watching them.

items.title: 'Good night, sweet dreams': Jacki Weaver leads farewells at Derryn Hinch state funeral

children.content: Broadcaster Ray Martin, Australian actress Jacki Weaver and performer Rhonda Burchmore are among the entertainment heavyweights to pay tribute to Derryn Hinch at his state funeral service.

gallery.altText: The stage and coffin of Derryn Hinch during a State Funeral Service for Derryn Hinch

items.title: Author Randa Abdel-Fattah sues Bendigo Writers Festival organisers

children.content: Randa Abdel-Fattah launches legal action after Bendigo Writers Festival organisers sent a code of conduct that sparked censorship concerns and led to the boycott of dozens of authors.

image.altText: A close-up photo of a woman's face

items.title: Plastic waste burning trial at cement factory prompts backlash

children.content: A major cement manufacturer has been allowed to burn more plastic waste for fuel at its Adelaide site in a decision a local council is seeking to challenge in court.

image.altText: A person outside the Adbri cement factory.

items.title: Wintry one-pot meals, from cheat's chicken soup to creamy butter beans

children.content: From butter beans with jammy leaks to a quick chicken soup, these recipes are for you if you're on hunt for a low-effort, tasty weeknight meal.

items.title: 'ISIS bride' Rayann El Houli granted bail by Melbourne court

children.content: A woman facing terror charges after living under Islamic State rule in Syria will be freed on bail, after being in custody for two months.

image.altText: Rayann El Houli July court sketch

items.title: Organisers of LGBT club in old church 'devastated' after lease terminated

children.content: Organisers of Divine Playhouse were served a breach notice earlier this month, in the wake of complaints from some members of the Christian community.

items.title: The 13 photos that summed up the chaos of the 2026 World Cup final

children.content: It was magical chaos from start to finish as Spain claimed victory in the 2026 World Cup final. Here are the 13 photos that summed it up.

articles.title: Spain's World Cup win a reminder collective spirit trumps solo stars

children.content: As some of football's biggest names lit up the World Cup, Spain flew under the radar all the way to hoisting the trophy on its final day.

articles.title: Tears streaming down his face, Messi leaves the World Cup stage a legend

children.content: As the champion wept on the pitch after the World Cup final defeat, Argentina may well have wondered with trepidation what life will look like in the post-Messi era.

items.title: Telehealth ban still standing between dying patients and their doctors

children.content: A federal law which threatens doctors if they use electronic communications, such as the internet or phone, to discuss voluntary assisted dying remains in force, even though telehealth is now an established part of healthcare.

image.altText: Andrew Denton holds a poster with a VAD law amendment at Parliament House beside VAD advocates Jason and Anthea Smith.

children.content: From Mexican wrestling to Indian slums, catch up on every week seven film from Race Around the World.

items.title: On the Tiwi Islands, where football is a religion, two teams lift the cup

children.content: The Imalu Tigers and Tapalinga Super Stars are the new premiers of Tiwi Islands football, claiming victory in the men's and women's competitions in electric grand final matches on Sunday.
